question: How to add a favicon to a website
description: Add favicons that look sharp on every device — from classic browser tabs to iOS home-screen icons and PWA manifests — with just a few <link> tags.

steps.title: Add the basic .ico favicon
steps.explanation: Place a favicon.ico at your site root — most browsers look there automatically. Add a <link> tag as well for explicit control over the path.
steps.code:
<head>
  <!-- Place favicon.ico at the site root for browsers that look there -->
  <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" sizes="32x32" />
</head>

steps.title: Use an SVG favicon for crispness at any size
steps.explanation: An SVG favicon scales perfectly on retina displays, supports dark-mode switching via CSS prefers-color-scheme, and is now supported in all modern browsers.
steps.code:
<link rel="icon" href="/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml" />
<!-- Fallback .ico for older browsers -->
<link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" sizes="32x32" />

steps.title: Add Apple touch icon for iOS
steps.explanation: When users add your site to their iOS home screen, Safari uses the apple-touch-icon. Provide a 180×180px PNG for best results.

steps.title: Add a web app manifest for PWAs and Android
steps.explanation: A manifest.json (or manifest.webmanifest) lets Android and desktop Chrome use your icons on the home screen / taskbar. Reference it with a <link> tag.

full_example.code:
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>My Site</title>

  <!-- SVG — scales perfectly, supports dark mode -->
  <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml" />
  <!-- .ico fallback for IE / older browsers -->
  <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" sizes="32x32" />
  <!-- 180×180 PNG for iOS home screen -->
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="/apple-touch-icon.png" sizes="180x180" />
  <!-- Web app manifest for Android / PWA -->
  <link rel="manifest" href="/manifest.webmanifest" />
  <!-- Browser toolbar / tile colour -->
  <meta name="theme-color" content="#1a1a1a" />
</head>
